ai_summary: Short pressure is heavily concentrated in mega-caps. SamsungElec alone scores 3.41, nearly double the next name, with 10 total high-pressure stocks suggesting systematic selling rather than broad distribution.

Price action strongly confirms short positioning. All 10 high-pressure names show rising short turnover above 5-day averages, and the score-weighted return of -2.08% aligns perfectly with the short thesis.

One name shows early divergence warning. SKBP scores 1.46 yet returns +0.20%, indicating shorts may face difficulty pushing price lower despite elevated turnover acceleration and foreign selling pressure.

Foreign and institutional outflows are driving the signal. The formula weights these factors at 0.40 combined, suggesting foreign players and domestic institutions are coordinating bearish positioning rather than retail short interest.

Squeeze risk remains minimal currently. Zero unconfirmed shorts across the universe means high-pressure names lack the price-pressure gradient needed for rapid covering, allowing shorts to maintain positions without immediate forced buyins.
